titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Clifton Ewing Hickok Papers
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11371118.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday March 28, 2021 at 03:02

did.unittitle: Clifton Ewing Hickok papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Correspondence, reports, charts, and maps created and compiled by Clifton Ewing Hickok related to water use and availability studies in southern California, as well as efforts by the Volcan Land and Water Company to sell its land and water rights to the city of San Diego, 1897-1915. The collection also includes reports and charts created by his business partner, William S. Post, and others, including Philip E. Harroun and Charles H. Lee.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Barry Lawrence Ruderman Antique Maps on the Thomas W. Streeter Fund, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Clifton Ewing Hickok Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Clifton Ewing Hickok Papers. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This finding aid was produced chiefly from a list provided by the dealer.

bioghist.head: Clifton Ewing Hickok (1880-1954)
bioghist.p: Major Clifton Ewing Hickok (1880-1954) was a civil engineer and city manager of Alameda, California. He was born in St. Joseph, Missouri. He earned a Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering from the University of California in 1908. In April 1912, he married visual artist Adelaide Valentine Cutler Hickok (1887-1987). They had a daughter, Beverly Hickok (born 1919). He worked as a civil engineer for several firms. From 1917 to 1921, he served as the city engineer for Alameda, California, except for years he served as an engineer with the United States Army during World War I, 1918-1919. In 1921, he became city manager of Alameda, California. He also served as the assistant Public Works Administrator of California during the Great Depression. In 1937, Hickok and his family moved to Berkeley, California.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, reports, charts, and maps created and compiled by Clifton Ewing Hickok related to water use and availability studies in southern California, as well as efforts by the Volcan Land and Water Co. to sell its land and water rights to the city of San Diego, 1897-1915. The collection also includes reports and charts created by his business partner, William S. Post, and others, including Philip E. Harroun and Charles H. Lee.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Papers, 1897-circa 1915. II. Oversize Papers, 1913-1915.

did.unittitle: Papers
Adams, Arthur L., San Francisco, California, letter to the Board of Town Trustees, Oceanside, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter serves as a report about the best means and cost for providing a water supply for Oceanside, California
Wrapper title: Report on Oceanside Project by Arthur L. Adams

did.unittitle: Southern California Mountain Water Company, Proposition of the Southern California Mountain Water Company to Sell a Portion of its System of Water Works to the City of San Diego, also to Lease Other Portions of its System of Water Works to the City of San Diego with the Option of Purchasing the Same
Post, William S., Report on Water Supply of the Bernardo Rancho
Lippincott, Joseph Barlow, letter to San Diego Chamber of Commerce, San Diego, California

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter serves as a report about a proposed bond issue for extensions and improvements to the municipal water system in San Diego, California

did.unittitle: Post, William S., Report on Cottonwood System of the City of San Diego
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Letter to William S. Post, about a letter by Joseph Barlow Lippincott to the San Diego Chamber of Commerce, San Diego, California

relatedmaterial.head: Related Archival Materials note
relatedmaterial.p: Associated materials: see Lippincott, Joseph Barlow, letter to San Diego Chamber of Commerce, San Diego, California, Box 1, folder 4

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter accompanied by notes that reference the report by Lippincott

did.unittitle: Harroun, Philip E., Report to the City of San Diego and the Volcan Land and Water Company on the Safe Net Yield Value Cost of Completed System and Cost of Water Delivered of the Properties of the Volcan Land and Water Company
Post, William S., Notes from a September 5, 1914, letter by Philip E. Harroun, that summarizes cost of constructing the Warner Dam, Carroll Dam, and pipeline from Carroll Dam to San Diego, California

Post, William S., and Clifton Ewing Hickok, Report of the Cost of Construction of Warner-Carroll-University Supply to the City of San Diego as Outlined in Offer of the Volcan Land and Water Company to the City of San Diego, Dated May 22, 1914
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Notes on Carroll Dam
Lee, Charles H., Preliminary Statement to the Volcan Land and Water Co. of Physical Situation on San Luis Rey River Relative to Injunction Suit Brought by City of Oceanside and Others

Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Notes on Charles Sumner Slichter, Field Measurements of the Rate of Movement of Underground Waters , United States Geological Survey, Water-Supply and Irrigation Paper; No. 140, 1905
Taylor, A. Movement of Underground Water
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Notes on Dam No. 1
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Notes on dams

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Dams include the Hume-Bennett Dam at Sanger, California; Big Meadows Dam in Mineral County, Colorado; Los Verjeles Dam in Yuba County, California; and Bear Valley Dam in Big Bear Valley, California

did.unittitle: Oversize Papers
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Cuyamaca Water Co., Types of Structures Contemplated for Replacement
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Volcan Land & Water Co., Mass Curve, Warner Reservoir (Based on Reservoir Half Full at Beginning)
Post, William S., Volcan Land & Water Co., Outlet Tower, Warner Reservoir
Post, William S., Volcan Land & Water Co., Cut and Fill Curves for Standard Canal on Ground Slopes of from 0° to 30°
Post, William S., Volcan Land & Water Co., Warner Dam

odd.head: General note
odd.p: One drawing has manuscript notes by Hickok

did.unittitle: Post, William S., Volcan Land & Water Co., Chart Showing Comparative Costs of Flume Types on Steep Slopes
Post, William S., Rainfall Charts, San Luis Rey River Drainage, San Ysabel River Drainage
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Volcan Land & Water Co., Warner Reservoir, Elevation Showing Formation Encountered in Outlet Tunnel
Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Volcan Land & Water Co., Mass Curve, Pamo Reservoir, Includes 15500 Acre Feet per annum From Warner Reservoir

title.part: Volcan Land & Water Co., Mass Curve Tabulations for Pamo and Sutherland Reservoirs (Combined)
Exhibit G, Plans of Types of Conduit Proposed Warner-Pamo Conduit of the Volcan Land and Water Company in the Cleveland National Forest, San Diego County, California

did.unittitle: Map that documents drainages, dams, reservoirs, and conduits in San Diego County.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Map constructed from topographic maps drafted by Richard Urquhart Goode, A. P. Davis, L. C. Fletcher, Joseph Barlow Lippincott, and T. G. Gerdine, and printed by the United States Geological Survey. Maps include the San Luis Rey Quadrangle (1901) and Ramona Quadrangle (1903)

did.unittitle: Hickok, Clifton Ewing, Cuyamaca Water Co., Mass Diagram for San Diego River & South Fork, Exclusive of Cuyamaca Combined with the Proposed Mountain Reservoirs at Diverting Dam, Tule, Cedar, Conejos, Poverty Gulch, and the Present Reservoirs at La Mesa, Murray Hill, Eucalyptus, for Period July 1st 1894 to July 1st 1914

title.part: Cuyamaca Water Company, Statistics of Undeveloped Reservoir Sites on San Diego River

did.unittitle: Post, William S., Volcan Land & Water Co., Carroll Reservoir Multiple Arch Dam Stress Sheet
